dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mapped flip-flop threshold logic to Bollinger Bands with 0.75 std dev (bb_upper/bb_lower). Entry long when price crosses above upper band upward; entry short when price crosses below lower band downward. Exit conditions are symmetric reversals: long exits on lower band crossdown, short exits on upper band crossup. This implements mutually exclusive threshold crossing states with automatic reversal on opposite threshold breach. Used bb_upper and bb_lower as they represent SMA ± standard deviations matching the signal's threshold definition.

This implements mutually exclusive threshold crossing states with automatic reversal on opposite threshold breach. Used bb_upper and bb_lower as they represent SMA ± standard deviations matching the signal's threshold definition.

source_rule_text: Use flip-flop logic with mutually exclusive threshold crossings to generate binary state transitions: maintain a directional signal until the opposite threshold is crossed in the opposite direction.
